result.summary: Google Cloud Access Context Manager exposes a REST API at `https://accesscontextmanager.googleapis.com/` and management commands through the `gcloud access-context-manager` CLI; both use Google Cloud OAuth-based authentication.
result.description: Access Context Manager is a Google Cloud security service for defining and managing attribute-based access controls such as access levels, service perimeters, and related policies. It is used to enforce context-aware access to Google Cloud resources and services.

gcp_oauth.label: Google Cloud OAuth 2.0 access token
gcp_oauth.setup: Use Google Cloud authentication to obtain an access token for your own Google Cloud principal. The easiest paths documented by Google are to sign in with the Google Cloud CLI via [`gcloud auth login`](https://docs.cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/auth/login) for user credentials, or use Application Default Credentials via [`gcloud auth application-default login`](https://docs.cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/auth/application-default/login). For this API, the REST method docs list the required OAuth scope as `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform`.
